vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1221: PAN-1148 follow-up — 2 minor review nits deferred at merge, plus a post-merge stream output panel regression discovered in UAT.

plan.title: PAN-1148 follow-up: 2 minor review nits deferred at merge
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: PAN-1148 shipped with two deferred review nits (F1: DELETE route emits the wrong lifecycle event after sharing a factory with POST /stop; F2: NaN-poisoning in FleetAgentsView avg-runtime reduce when startedAt is invalid) and a post-merge UAT regression (F3: DrawerActiveAgent stream excerpt panel renders 'No recent stream output' for agents that are actively producing output, contrary to PRD §4.7.8).
narratives.Proposal: Three independent beads, one per finding. F1 parameterizes the lifecycle event name in the shared agentStopRoute factory so DELETE re-emits agent.delete_requested while POST /stop keeps agent.stop_requested. F2 adds Number.isFinite guards inside the avg-runtime reduce and divides by the count of finite entries so a single bad startedAt no longer poisons the metric. F3 walks the agent-output data flow from server emission through WS subscription to the selectAgentOutput consumer, fixes the broken link at its source (no defensive shims), and adds a Playwright e2e to lock in the behavior. Each bead is shippable and reviewable in isolation; no hard ordering between them.
narratives.OutOfScope: The substrate gaps that caused PAN-1148's churn cycle (kimi-k2.6 rate-limit halts, correctness-reviewer recurring timeouts, test-passed-via-skip → no-ship-auto-dispatch gap) are tracked separately under PAN-1215 and other follow-ups and are not addressed here.

autoDecisions.summary: Include F3 (stream output panel regression) as a third bead in scope.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue body was amended on 2026-05-19 with the F3 update explicitly tagged 'Severity: User-visible regression on the merged redesign's headline feature. Worth fixing before the next ship.' Keeping it in this issue avoids splintering follow-ups and matches the user's update intent.

autoDecisions.summary: F1 shipped as a single bead that parameterizes the shared agentStopRoute factory (third arg for the lifecycle event name) rather than splitting the routes back into two near-identical handlers.
autoDecisions.rationale: Splitting would duplicate ~40 lines of identical body (eventStore append, activity emit, cache invalidation). Parameterizing is the smaller diff and preserves DRY without losing observable distinction. The issue's recommended fix lists both approaches; pick the cheaper one.

autoDecisions.summary: F2 also fixes the divisor (count of finite-duration agents) instead of only guarding the reducer.
autoDecisions.rationale: A pure guard without divisor adjustment understates the average proportional to how many invalid entries there are — a still-broken metric, just a different broken value. The hazards section explicitly tests the all-invalid case to ensure it returns 0 (matches the existing empty-array branch).

autoDecisions.summary: F3 includes a Playwright e2e test that seeds agentOutputById via store.setState rather than relying on a live agent.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue's stated minimum bar includes a Playwright test. Existing patterns in src/dashboard/frontend/tests/*.spec.ts (e.g. command-deck-resource-strip.spec.ts) stub the dashboard store via page.evaluate — same shape used here.

autoDecisions.summary: Every bead ships with a regression test (server route test for F1, FleetAgentsView unit test for F2, Playwright e2e for F3).
autoDecisions.rationale: PAN-1148 cycle-15/16 review history shows the original gaps shipped without test coverage and re-surfaced as nits. Adding tests is the cheapest way to prevent a third recurrence.

autoDecisions.summary: All three beads default metadata.requiresInspection to false.
autoDecisions.rationale: None of the three beads lays foundation, defines a public API, encodes a cross-cutting protocol, or touches a security boundary. F1 is a string parameter addition. F2 is a numeric guard. F3 is a discrete data-flow bug fix in one panel. The work.inspect gate would have no judgment-call defects to catch.

autoDecisions.summary: No hard 'blocks' edges between the three beads.
autoDecisions.rationale: Each bead touches different files (agents.ts server route vs FleetAgentsView.tsx frontend vs DrawerActiveAgent.tsx / store wiring). Ordering is mechanically irrelevant; the work agent can land them in any order or in parallel sub-commits within one PR.

items.title: Restore agent.delete_requested lifecycle event for DELETE /api/agents/:id

narrative.Action: Parameterize the lifecycle event name in src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts:1053 agentStopRoute(method, path) by adding a third argument `event: 'agent.delete_requested' | 'agent.stop_requested'`. Pass 'agent.delete_requested' from the DELETE construction (line 1099) and 'agent.stop_requested' from the POST /stop construction (line 1103). Before changing, grep both event names across src/ and packages/ to confirm no downstream consumer was relying on the merged behavior; if a consumer was, update it to subscribe to both (do NOT silently re-alias). Add a server route test that asserts each method produces the correct lifecycle event in the log.

subItems.title: agentStopRoute factory accepts a lifecycle event name argument and uses it in the appendAgentLifecycleLog call (no hardcoded 'agent.stop_requested')
PR description documents the grep result for both event names and confirms either (a) no downstream consumer breaks, or (b) which consumers were updated to subscribe to both
New server route test in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/ asserts DELETE emits 'agent.delete_requested' and POST /stop emits 'agent.stop_requested' to the lifecycle log
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test all pass from the workspace root

items.title: Guard FleetAgentsView avg-runtime reduce against NaN startedAt

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Agents/FleetAgentsView.tsx:290, replace the unguarded reduce over runningAgents with a version that computes `new Date(agent.startedAt).getTime()` once per agent, skips entries where the result is not Number.isFinite (covering undefined/null/empty/malformed startedAt and the NaN return from invalid Date), accumulates only finite durations, and divides by the count of finite durations (not runningAgents.length). When zero agents have finite durations, return 0 to match the existing empty-array branch. Update FleetAgentsView.test.tsx with three new cases.

subItems.title: Reduce in FleetAgentsView.tsx:290 skips any running agent whose `new Date(agent.startedAt).getTime()` is not Number.isFinite
avgRuntime is the sum of finite durations divided by the count of finite-duration agents; returns 0 (not NaN, not Infinity) when no running agent has a finite startedAt

FleetAgentsView.test.tsx adds three regression cases: (a) all valid startedAt (avg matches), (b) mixed valid+invalid (avg uses only valid entries and is finite), (c) all invalid startedAt (avgRuntime tile renders the formatted 0)
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test all pass from the workspace root

items.title: Fix DrawerActiveAgent stream excerpt panel showing 'No recent stream output' for active agents

narrative.Action: Reproduce the regression first: start the dashboard, open IssueDrawer for an issue whose work/plan/review agent is actively emitting output (the issue body cites PAN-1052), and confirm DrawerActiveAgent.tsx renders 'No recent stream output' despite the agent being live. Walk back the data flow until the broken link is identified: (1) does selectAgentOutput(activeAgent.id) return data? (2) is `agentOutputById[activeAgent.id]` populated in the store? (3) is the WS subscription to agent.output_received delivering events for that agent (see src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts:524 and packages/contracts/src/event-reducers.ts:431)? (4) is the agent key the server uses ('agent-pan-1052' style) the same key the component looks up? Fix at the broken link only — no defensive shims downstream. Document the root cause in the PR body. Add a Playwright e2e test in src/dashboard/frontend/tests/ that stubs agentOutputById and asserts the stream-excerpt panel renders the seeded lines (not the empty-state fallback).

subItems.title: Repro confirmed: PR body documents the exact reproduction steps and shows the diagnosed root cause (which step in server-emit → WS-subscribe → store-reduce → selector-read was dropping the data, and why)
Fix is applied at the broken link itself, not as a defensive workaround downstream. PR body explicitly addresses the CLAUDE.md 'No Bandaids' rule and explains why the chosen fix is at the root cause.

After the fix, opening the drawer for an active agent that is producing output renders the agent's last N output lines in the stream-excerpt panel (data-testid='drawer-active-agent-stream') instead of the 'No recent stream output' empty-state

New Playwright e2e test in src/dashboard/frontend/tests/ uses an isolated browser instance, seeds useDashboardStore with at least one active agent and at least 8 lines in agentOutputById for that agent's id, opens the drawer, and asserts data-testid='drawer-active-agent-stream' contains the seeded lines (the last 8) and does NOT contain the 'No recent stream output' empty-state text
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test all pass; the new Playwright spec passes against the local dashboard build
